title: Grok Build Bridge configuration
description: Strict schema for a bridge.yaml document. Every field exists to keep generated agents deterministic, auditable, and cheap to reproduce across CI runs.

version.description: Schema version. Pinned to an enum so a future v1.1/v2 cannot be silently consumed by an older bridge binary.

name.description: Slug used for the build directory, the X handle suffix, and the deploy target name. Restricted to DNS/slug-safe characters because downstream systems (Vercel/Render/X) reject anything else.

description.description: Human-readable summary. Capped at 280 characters so a release can be announced in a single X post without truncation.

build.description: How the agent source is produced.

source.description: Where the code comes from. `local` reads an existing tree; `grok` calls xAI; `grok-build-cli` shells out to the ecosystem CLI.

grok_prompt.description: Prompt handed to Grok when `source` is `grok` or `grok-build-cli`. Required there, optional for `local`; cross-field enforcement lives in `allOf`.

language.description: Target language for the generated agent. Enum keeps the builder's templates honest; new languages need an explicit schema bump.

entrypoint.description: Relative path that the runtime executes. Default is resolved language-by-language by the parser because JSON Schema cannot express a cross-field default.

required_tools.description: xAI tools the agent is allowed to call. Explicit allow-list so the safety layer can deny everything else by default.

deploy.description: Where and how the built agent is shipped.

target.description: Deployment target. `x` hands off to grok-install's `deploy_to_x`; the others are glue for non-X hosts. `railway` and `flyio` write a config file (railway.json / fly.toml) and shell out to the host CLI when it is on PATH.

runtime.description: Runtime glue used to deploy. Kept as a free-form string so ecosystem plugins can register new runtimes without schema churn.

post_to_x.description: If true, the agent will post autonomously after deploy. Defaults to false so dry-runs never reach the public timeline.

safety_scan.description: Whether to run the safety pipeline before deploy. Defaults to true; turning it off should be an explicit, reviewable choice.

schedule.description: Optional cron-ish schedule string. Not validated as a cron expression here because dialects vary across hosts; the chosen runtime validates it.

model.description: Grok model id. Enum pinned to the models the Bridge has been tested against so a typo (e.g. 'grok-4') fails fast.

reasoning_effort.description: Reasoning budget dial. Mirrors the xAI API's `reasoning_effort` so the parser can pass it through unchanged.

personality.description: System-prompt personality snippet. Capped at 500 characters to protect the prompt budget and keep the generated agent's voice focused.

safety.description: Runtime safety knobs. Optional because sensible defaults should cover most users.

audit_before_post.description: Run a Grok-in-the-loop audit before publishing any content. On by default because the Bridge's whole value prop is 'safely deployed'.

max_tokens_per_run.description: Ceiling on tokens per single bridge run. Bounded so a runaway loop cannot burn the xAI budget. Min/max chosen to cover real agents without enabling abuse.

lucas_veto_enabled.description: Enable the Lucas veto gate. The Orchestra project wires this up; in the Bridge it stays off by default to avoid a hard dependency on Orchestra.

allOf.description: grok_prompt is mandatory whenever the build `source` is backed by Grok.
